body:
This small PR refactors the interface for specifying the type of `similarity_profile` (eg how detailed we would like to be when computing the correction to a logarthmic profile). This allows us to have a "complete" similarity profile and an approximate one that's used in COARE.

There's still a bit of work and/or thinking required to flesh out the interaction between specifying the similarity profile vs specifying the stability functions. For example, if we want to use the [Nishizawa and Kitamura 2018](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1029/2018MS001534) similarity profiles (which are more appropriate for coupling to a finite volume atmosphere), we don't need the stability functions themselves but rather their integral over the cell thickness:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aa0cca2f-89b4-4655-95d7-99e299a8b2a4)
title: Change bulk coefficients to similarity profile
